build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: The deployment loop is playable: DP accumulates over time, unit cards display costs, the player can drag or select a unit and place it on a legal tile to create a defender, successful placement subtracts DP, and illegal placement or insufficient DP fails without placing the unit. Score 1 only if all parts work through player input in one demonstrated flow. Score 0 if units can be placed for free, anywhere, or only through scripted spawning. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Enemies follow authored paths from spawn to base endpoint, defenders engage enemies within range reducing HP on both sides, and units at zero HP are removed from the field. Enemies that reach the endpoint reduce base life. Score 1 only if pathing, combat with HP loss, unit death/removal, and leak penalty all function together. Score 0 if enemies wander randomly, ignore defenders, have no HP, or combat is only decorative. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Enemies arrive in multiple discrete waves with pacing changes between them, later waves increase pressure through quantity, type, speed, or HP, and the wave system ends in victory only after the final wave is cleared. Score 1 only if at least two distinct waves and a final-wave victory condition are observable. Score 0 if all enemies spawn at once, waves are label-only with no gameplay difference, or victory is unrelated to clearing waves. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Campaign flow is complete: base life reaching zero triggers defeat, clearing a stage marks progress and unlocks the next, save/load can restore completed-stage state, and retry/back navigation works without relaunching. Score 1 only if victory, defeat, stage progression, and load behavior are all demonstrated or clearly reachable. Score 0 if the game has no loss condition, no persistent progress, or disconnected menus. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least three deployable unit types with distinct silhouettes/icons and distinct tactical roles (e.g., blocker, ranged damage, splash, slow/support, high-cost elite). Differences must include gameplay-relevant stats or behavior, not just names or recolors. Score 1 only if the card UI presents all types with cost and role cues. Score 0 if there is one unit type or all units behave identically. Score 0.5 at most if only labels or static icons distinguish types with no observable role difference during battle. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least three enemy types appear across waves, visually distinct and functionally different through HP, speed, armor, swarm count, flying behavior, or leak value. Enemy variety must change defender placement or priority decisions. Score 1 only if different enemy types demand different tactical responses. Score 0 if enemies are clones or tint swaps. Score 0.5 at most if only labels or decorative differences distinguish enemy types with no gameplay impact. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Multiple stages or maps are selectable from a stage screen, with at least two visually and tactically distinct layouts offering different path geometry, deployable zones, terrain theme, or enemy composition. Score 1 only if entering different stages changes the battle experience. Score 0 if there is only one map or all stages reuse the same layout with cosmetic recolor. Score 0.5 at most if only labels or decorative elements distinguish stages with no tactical difference. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

An additional strategic layer beyond baseline placement exists, such as unit upgrades, cooldown skills, directional facing, terrain bonuses, economy tradeoffs, or unlocks after clearing stages. Score 1 only if this layer is usable and visibly affects decisions during play. Score 0 if all content is a single static battle with no strategic progression or choice beyond dropping units. Score 0.5 at most if only static text or disconnected UI elements suggest a deeper system with no observable gameplay effect. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Battlefield readability is strong during play: enemy path, deployable tiles, blocked tiles, base endpoint, spawn point, defenders, enemies, and current engagements are distinguishable at 1280x720. A player can understand where units may go and where enemies will travel without reading source code. Score 0 if the path or deployment zones blend into the background or are only implied by labels. Score 0.5 at most if only labels or text indicate zones with no visual distinction. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Essential game state is always readable: DP counter, wave number/progress, base life, unit costs, and battle result are visible in stable, non-overlapping UI regions. Score 1 only if state remains readable throughout battle and result scenes. Score 0 if any core counter is missing, hidden behind interaction, overlaps other UI, or appears only transiently. Score 0.5 at most if only static disconnected labels show state with no clear HUD framing. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Player action feedback is clear: selecting or dragging a card shows selection/ghost/range feedback, valid placement produces visible confirmation, invalid placement or insufficient DP produces an unmistakable failure cue, attacks show projectile/impact/contact feedback, and leaks reduce base life with a visible cue. Score 1 only if success and failure feedback are both observable. Score 0 if actions happen silently or only counters change with no visual response. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The interface remains usable as a game surface: card hand, stage select, battle HUD, pause/retry/result controls are legible, framed, and do not cover critical battlefield action. Score 1 only if all major screens have professional spacing and contrast. Score 0 if UI elements clip, overlap, hide enemies/paths, or look like debug text scattered over gameplay. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The game has a coherent art direction: title, stage select, battlefield, units, enemies, effects, and UI share a consistent visual style and palette. Screenshots read as an authored vertical slice. Score 0 if the game relies on raw ColorRect shapes, default Godot grey controls, mismatched asset packs, or bright placeholder colors with no unifying theme.

Maps and environmental art are finished: battlefield tiles have texture or surface detail, paths look like real roads/tracks/pipes, deployable zones are integrated into the art, and distinct maps have distinct visual identities. Score 1 only if the world art supports the campaign fantasy. Score 0 if maps are flat fills, plain rectangles, or the same empty board repeated. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Defender and enemy sprites are real game art with readable silhouettes, not primitives. Unit cards include icon art and costs; enemies show type identity at gameplay scale; attack effects, death effects, and leak effects match the style. Score 1 only if units and enemies could plausibly ship as final sprites. Score 0 if any primary unit or enemy type is represented by plain boxes/circles or undifferentiated recolors. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

UI and motion polish meet a shippable bar: title/stage-select screens, HUD chrome, result overlays, card hover/drag, projectile/attack animation, death animation, and scene transitions use custom styling and multi-frame animation or tweening. Score 1 only if polish is visible across menus and battle. Score 0 if major screens use naked default Label/Button widgets or hard-cut/snap without authored motion. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
